title: steriphoma paradoxum
parts: Steriphoma paradoxum has many medicinal parts, such as its roots and leaves, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The roots of Steriphoma paradoxum are known to contain triterpenoid saponins, while the leaves contain phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The plant's roots are also rich in bioactive compounds like alkaloids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Steriphoma paradoxum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions in some individuals, which can manifest as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, potentially causing dehydration if left untreated. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can cause headaches, dizziness, and confusion, impairing cognitive function and judgment. In rare cases, improper use of Steriphoma paradoxum can also trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
